title: Mac Is a Serial Killer

jokes.text: Describing an episode of Law and Order in full detail is not a story.
jokes.setup: Character is enthusiastically describing Law and Order
jokes.punchline: Dennis deadpan dismissal of this as a non-story
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Dennis's pretentious dismissal of his friend's enthusiasm. Economy and delivery make it work.

jokes.text: You want to be a lawyer? Don't you think you should know how to read or write?
jokes.setup: Charlie claims he could be a good lawyer
jokes.punchline: Dennis points out Charlie can't read or write
jokes.explanation: Classic character-based humor - Charlie's delusional confidence meets Dennis's brutal honesty about Charlie's illiteracy.

jokes.text: Objection. Hearsay. That's lawyer talk. That's the kind of stuff you have to do.
jokes.setup: Charlie being called out for not knowing how to read/write
jokes.punchline: Charlie uses random legal terms as if that proves his competence
jokes.explanation: Charlie's confident misunderstanding of how law works - he thinks knowing legal jargon equals competence.

jokes.text: Why? What are you worried about? You don't fit the description.
jokes.setup: Dee worried about serial killer targeting young attractive blondes
jokes.punchline: Mac implies she doesn't fit that description
jokes.explanation: Standard roast setup but works because it's Mac's casual cruelty and establishes the running gag about Dee's appearance.

jokes.text: You're like a female Larry Bird. There's too much of you.
jokes.setup: Dee insisting she fits the victim profile
jokes.punchline: Mac's specific and weird comparison to Larry Bird
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific insult - comparing Dee to Larry Bird is unexpected and weirdly specific in a way that's both mean and absurd.

jokes.text: He would never finish the job. He'd get the job done. He'd spend his whole night hacking and sawing.
jokes.setup: Mac explaining why serial killer wouldn't target Dee
jokes.punchline: Elaborately describing the logistical challenges of murdering Dee
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark escalation - Mac commits fully to the premise of why Dee would be inconvenient to murder, making it funnier and more disturbing.

jokes.text: How many people have I murdered tonight? How is this just one person?
jokes.setup: Continuing the premise about Dee being hard to murder
jokes.punchline: Imagining the killer's confusion at Dee's size
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - the killer questioning his own body count because Dee is so big. Perfect dark absurdity.

jokes.text: That's okay, 'cause Larry Bird's an excellent player. He's an awesome player.
jokes.setup: Mac calls Dee 'Larry Bird' as an insult
jokes.punchline: Dee completely misses the insult and focuses on Larry Bird's basketball skills
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Dee's obliviousness to insults and ability to find the positive in being compared to a male athlete.

jokes.text: What's up, bitches? Hey yo!
jokes.setup: Mac enters with overly confident greeting
jokes.punchline: The awkward forced coolness of the delivery
jokes.explanation: Standard Mac trying-too-hard moment but sets up the interrogation that follows.

jokes.text: These are different clothes. Where'd you get those scratches on your neck?
jokes.setup: Friends suspiciously interrogating Mac
jokes.punchline: Each question gets more incriminating despite Mac's denials
jokes.explanation: Good escalating interrogation where each denial makes Mac look guiltier. Classic sitcom structure.

jokes.text: Serial killing.
jokes.setup: Someone asks what's gotten into Mac lately
jokes.punchline: Frank's matter-of-fact response about serial killing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank moment - treating serial killing as a casual diagnosis. Economy is perfect, delivery sells it.

jokes.text: You can't just torture someone 'cause you think they're guilty. Oh, don't give me that liberal bullshit.
jokes.setup: Charlie objects to torturing Mac
jokes.punchline: Frank dismisses basic human rights as 'liberal bullshit'
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank - treating torture as a political issue rather than a moral one. Perfect character voice.

jokes.text: He's not smart enough. He's a dumb person. And dumb people are not capable of serial killing.
jokes.setup: Dennis defending Mac from serial killer accusations
jokes.punchline: His defense is that Mac is too stupid to be a serial killer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis logic - defending Mac by insulting his intelligence. Shows how these friends treat each other.

jokes.text: Let's carve our best friend to pieces with a chain saw based on a hunch.
jokes.setup: Frank's suggestion to torture Mac based on suspicion
jokes.punchline: Charlie's sarcastic summary of how extreme Frank's plan is
jokes.explanation: Charlie being the voice of reason by pointing out the absurdity of Frank's violence, which is itself absurd.

jokes.text: Sorry, Larry Bird.
jokes.setup: Dennis addressing Dee
jokes.punchline: Casually calling her Larry Bird as if it's her name now
jokes.explanation: Good callback usage - treating the insult nickname as casual address. Shows how nicknames stick in this group.

jokes.text: I forgot about this thing that I gotta do, take care of. I was thinking about it in the bathroom.
jokes.setup: Mac trying to leave suspiciously
jokes.punchline: Mac's obviously improvised excuse
jokes.explanation: Mac's terrible lying skills on full display with this rambling non-excuse

jokes.text: Because of your... Oh, God. Situation down there.
jokes.setup: Mac explaining why he can't be public about their relationship
jokes.punchline: His awkward euphemism for Carmen being transgender
jokes.explanation: Mac's discomfort with being direct about Carmen's anatomy creates authentic cringe comedy.

jokes.text: You mean my penis? Well, you don't have to say it out loud.
jokes.setup: Mac using euphemisms about Carmen's anatomy
jokes.punchline: Carmen says it directly while Mac is horrified by the directness
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Carmen's directness and Mac's squeamishness about his own situation.

jokes.text: But then things start to get hot and heavy and you scratch me. Which is cool. But you're really strong, and it hurts.
jokes.setup: Mac explaining their physical relationship
jokes.punchline: TMI about their sexual encounters and Mac being hurt by Carmen
jokes.explanation: Mac's awkward oversharing about their sex life while trying to be diplomatic about Carmen being stronger.

jokes.text: And sometimes... just sometimes... I bump up against it.
jokes.setup: Mac describing physical issues with Carmen
jokes.punchline: His euphemistic way of describing contact with Carmen's penis
jokes.explanation: Mac's delicate phrasing about an awkward sexual reality. The pauses make it funnier.

jokes.text: Hold it closer to your face and look really crazy. Like I'm killing somebody?
jokes.setup: Frank taking pictures with a chainsaw to look menacing
jokes.punchline: Charlie innocently asks if he should pose like he's murdering someone
jokes.explanation: Charlie's innocent enthusiasm for posing like a killer while they're investigating actual murders.

jokes.text: Put your tongue closer to the blade.
jokes.setup: Frank directing Charlie's chainsaw photo pose
jokes.punchline: Frank wanting increasingly dangerous photo poses
jokes.explanation: Frank's casual endangerment of Charlie for a better photo. Dark comedy with perfect character dynamics.

jokes.text: You do not fit the profile. I fit the profile, Dennis!
jokes.setup: Dennis saying Dee doesn't fit the victim profile
jokes.punchline: Dee's desperate insistence that she does fit
jokes.explanation: Dee's desperation to be considered attractive enough to be a murder victim is perfectly absurd character moment.

jokes.text: I have a down jacket on, a wool blanket. It's a very toasty situation I got back here.
jokes.setup: Dee complaining about being cold while acting as bait
jokes.punchline: Dennis is bundled up and comfortable while Dee suffers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis selfishness - comfortable while his sister is the one in danger and discomfort.

jokes.text: What's the decorating motif of this place... prison cell?
jokes.setup: Frank commenting on Mac's apartment decor
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to a prison cell
jokes.explanation: Frank's blunt observation about Mac's spartan living situation. Sets up Mac's sad lifestyle.

jokes.text: You think I want some Jew lawyer crawling up my ass in six months?
jokes.setup: Frank explaining why he's wearing gloves
jokes.punchline: Frank's casually racist explanation about avoiding lawyers
jokes.explanation: Frank's casual racism is consistent with his character but relies on shock value rather than wit. Lower craft score for lazy writing.

jokes.text: Objection. Extremely racist, man.
jokes.setup: Frank's racist comment
jokes.punchline: Charlie treating it like a legal objection
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Charlie's lawyer obsession while calling out Frank's racism in legal terms.

jokes.text: And Bingo was his nameo
jokes.setup: Frank finding evidence about human anatomy
jokes.punchline: Frank's bizarre victory celebration referencing a children's song
jokes.explanation: Frank's weird celebration with a children's song is perfectly absurd and unexpected. Great character moment.

jokes.text: I woke up to you doing some pretty frantic research last night, pal.
jokes.setup: Charlie defending that all guys research their own genitalia
jokes.punchline: Charlie implying Frank was frantically researching his own genitals
jokes.explanation: Charlie turning Frank's accusation around with an uncomfortable revelation about Frank's nighttime activities.

jokes.text: Damn, girl. Who you with?
jokes.setup: Dee acting as bait on the street
jokes.punchline: A pimp immediately approaching her
jokes.explanation: The immediate switch from serial killer bait to prostitute situation is unexpected and fits the show's escalation pattern.

jokes.text: Maybe if you get up off that crack rock, you can come and be Pepper Jacks best ho.
jokes.setup: Pepper Jack propositioning Dee
jokes.punchline: His specific phrasing and referring to himself in third person
jokes.explanation: Pepper Jack's third-person speech pattern and specific slang creates a memorable character voice.

jokes.text: But that stuff, you gotta be careful because it'll mess you. Why are you asking? Do you have some on you?
jokes.setup: Dee trying to explain her past drug use
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking if he has drugs after warning about them
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dee moment - giving a warning about drugs then immediately asking for them. Shows her lack of self-awareness.

jokes.text: No. Well, technically, we drove here together. But I'm not with anybody.
jokes.setup: Pepper Jack asking if Dee is with Dennis
jokes.punchline: Dee's technical clarification while missing the implication
jokes.explanation: Dee's literal-minded response while not understanding she's being claimed by a pimp.

jokes.text: Pepper Jack trying to conduct some business here too. You with this cracker?
jokes.setup: Dennis interrupting Pepper Jack's conversation with Dee
jokes.punchline: Pepper Jack refers to Dennis as a cracker while talking about conducting business
jokes.explanation: Good character voice - Pepper Jack's casual racism while claiming territory

jokes.text: Do Pepper Jack look like he playing? I don't think Pepper Jacks playing.
jokes.setup: Threat escalating with Pepper Jack
jokes.punchline: The repetitive third-person speech pattern getting more menacing
jokes.explanation: The repetitive speech pattern becomes menacing while maintaining its absurdity. Good escalation.

jokes.text: Is that Fraggle Rock? Yeah. Oh, Pepper Jack love Fraggle Rock.
jokes.setup: Tense negotiation with threatening pimp
jokes.punchline: Pepper Jack's enthusiasm for children's show thermos
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion - menacing pimp becomes childlike over Fraggle Rock. Brilliant character contradiction that works perfectly.

jokes.text: We need to find somebody who we would want to kill. Here you go, Dee.
jokes.setup: Dennis and Charlie discussing finding a victim to understand the killer
jokes.punchline: Immediately identifying the waitress, then Dee
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation where the theoretical exercise immediately becomes targeting real people. Perfect timing and structure.

jokes.text: And shithead. Hope you burn your tongue.
jokes.setup: Waitress serving Dennis and Charlie
jokes.punchline: Her casual hostility toward Dennis
jokes.explanation: Waitress's specific hostility toward Dennis shows their history and her justified hatred.

jokes.text: But it's like a drug. I just get wrapped up in the moment and boom, it just happens.
jokes.setup: Mac confessing to Charlie about his problem
jokes.punchline: Describing his relationship with Carmen like an addiction while Charlie thinks it's murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect double meaning where Mac describes his sexual relationship in terms that sound like murder confession.

jokes.text: Well, that's good. She won't be a problem. She ain't gonna tell anybody.
jokes.setup: Mac saying only Carmen knows about 'it'
jokes.punchline: Charlie assumes Mac will kill Carmen to silence her
jokes.explanation: Charlie's casual assumption that Mac will murder Carmen to keep her quiet. Dark comedy from the misunderstanding.

jokes.text: Deny, deny, deny, deny. You just deny it. Deny it to the end. If you deny something, it means it never happened.
jokes.setup: Charlie giving Mac legal advice
jokes.punchline: Charlie's complete misunderstanding of how denial works legally
jokes.explanation: Charlie's magical thinking about denial is perfect - he thinks denying something makes it not exist.

jokes.text: And then I do some moves. What the hell does that have anything to do with what we're talking about?
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining his legal strategy
jokes.punchline: Charlie starts doing random movements and Mac's confusion
jokes.explanation: Charlie's random physical movements as 'lawyer stuff' is perfectly absurd and confuses even Mac.

jokes.text: My neighbor Gary works here. He is desperately in love with me.
jokes.setup: Dee explaining how to get free tools
jokes.punchline: Her delusional belief about Gary's feelings
jokes.explanation: Classic Dee delusion about men being interested in her, used here for criminal purposes.

jokes.text: Who's your guy? Psycho clown.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining his serial killer persona
jokes.punchline: Dee's choice of psycho clown
jokes.explanation: Dee choosing the most cliche serial killer persona shows her lack of imagination.

jokes.text: These shears, they do cut through bone, yes? Uh, yeah. I guess.
jokes.setup: Dee asking Gary about gardening shears
jokes.punchline: She's asking if they cut through bone while Gary awkwardly answers
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment - Gary's uncomfortable compliance with obviously suspicious questions

jokes.text: Oh, man. That guy's a pussy. Yeah, spineless.
jokes.setup: Gary giving them free tools because Dee smiled at him
jokes.punchline: Dennis and Dee's contempt for Gary's kindness
jokes.explanation: Shows how Dennis and Dee treat people who are nice to them with contempt. Classic character trait.

jokes.text: Move to strike. Wait a minute. What are you talking about? Ask her fair questions!
jokes.setup: Charlie defending Mac's mom with legal objections
jokes.punchline: Charlie getting confused about his own legal strategy
jokes.explanation: Charlie gets confused about whether he's defending or prosecuting. Perfect Charlie logic failure.

jokes.text: You think your son's out there killing people? Yeah.
jokes.setup: Charlie asking Mac's mom the direct question
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Mac's immediate casual 'yeah'
jokes.explanation: Perfect punchline - Mac's own mother casually agreeing he's a serial killer. The economy and timing are perfect.

jokes.text: John Wayne Gacy? He was a psycho clown. He was an excellent one. Did good work.
jokes.setup: Dennis dismissing psycho clown serial killers as movie fiction
jokes.punchline: Dee's admiring assessment of Gacy's 'work'
jokes.explanation: Dee's professional assessment of a serial killer's 'work' is perfectly dark and matter-of-fact.

jokes.text: Oh, shit. That's a bummer. Yeah, you're right.
jokes.setup: Dennis realizing they can't actually murder someone
jokes.punchline: Dennis's disappointment about not being able to commit murder
jokes.explanation: Dennis's genuine disappointment about not being able to murder someone shows his sociopathic tendencies.

jokes.text: if you hadn't been squeaking around like a goddamn psycho clown... she never would've noticed us.
jokes.setup: Their stalking attempt failed
jokes.punchline: Dennis blaming Dee's clown costume for ruining their stealth
jokes.explanation: Dennis blaming their failure on Dee's obvious costume choice. Good callback to earlier setup.

jokes.text: I know who the killer is. You do? Yes. Oh, my God. Dee, the real serial killer is Mac. That's what I just said.
jokes.setup: Dennis having a realization about the killer's identity
jokes.punchline: Dee pointing out she literally just said the same thing
jokes.explanation: Dennis's obliviousness to what was just said and taking credit for Dee's idea. Classic sibling dynamic.

jokes.text: Are you shitting me? Now, Dee, can you do a girl's voice?
jokes.setup: Frank asking if Dee can do a girl's voice for the phone trap
jokes.punchline: The implication that Dee doesn't normally sound like a girl
jokes.explanation: Frank questioning whether Dee can sound feminine is a perfect insult disguised as a practical question.

jokes.text: I'm not ashamed of you. I'm ashamed of myself.
jokes.setup: Carmen accusing Mac of being ashamed of her
jokes.punchline: Mac's attempt to be diplomatic that comes off worse
jokes.explanation: Mac's attempt to be nice actually makes things worse. Perfect Mac relationship incompetence.

jokes.text: When did you start hearing voices? That could actually help us.
jokes.setup: Mac saying 'she won't talk to me anymore'
jokes.punchline: Dennis thinks Mac is hearing voices, sees it as a legal defense
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - Dennis misunderstands and immediately sees opportunity

jokes.text: Uh, more ripped. Strike that. Jacked. Irrelevant. Toned. Exaggeration. I work out. I'll allow it.
jokes.setup: Mac describing himself on the phone while Charlie provides legal commentary
jokes.punchline: The back and forth about Mac's physique with legal objections
jokes.explanation: Perfect combination of Mac's vanity about his body and Charlie treating it like a legal proceeding. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Because drawing a confession out of someone... is like doing a beautiful dance... a beautiful dance with a chain saw.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining why he needs the chainsaw
jokes.punchline: Frank's poetic description of torture as a beautiful dance
jokes.explanation: Frank's romantic description of chainsaw torture is beautifully absurd and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Ooh, not really into the whole condom thing.
jokes.setup: Fake Sandy asking if Mac brought condoms
jokes.punchline: Mac casually admits to unsafe sex practices
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate irresponsibility from Mac

jokes.text: Oh. Why hello, Mac. Not so young and attractive, is she?
jokes.setup: Mac expecting to meet a young attractive woman named Sandy
jokes.punchline: Dee revealing herself as 'Sandy'
jokes.explanation: Good reveal of the trap with Dennis immediately insulting Dee's appearance. Solid callback structure.

jokes.text: I've been banging the tranny! I didn't want you guys to find out.
jokes.setup: Everyone thinking Mac is a serial killer
jokes.punchline: Mac's confession reveals the real reason for his suspicious behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection payoff - all the suspicious behavior had an innocent (but embarrassing) explanation. Great structure and revelation.

jokes.text: What? Ew. Oh. I don't even know how that works. It's complicated. There's a lot... She tapes it back.
jokes.setup: Mac's confession about his relationship
jokes.punchline: Everyone's disgusted reactions and Mac's clinical explanation
jokes.explanation: Good uncomfortable reaction sequence - everyone processing Mac's revelation

jokes.text: It's complicated. There's a lot... She tapes it back.
jokes.setup: Friends asking how the relationship works physically
jokes.punchline: Mac's uncomfortable explanation about Carmen's anatomy
jokes.explanation: Mac's awkward oversharing about Carmen's anatomy creates appropriate cringe comedy.

jokes.text: No. There's a bunch of severed heads in there. What? There's about 15 severed heads in there, I'd say.
jokes.setup: Frank asking if there's beer in the fridge
jokes.punchline: Dee casually reporting multiple severed heads
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy escalation - the casual discovery of actual murder evidence after all their fake investigation. Brilliant structure.

jokes.text: What are you people doing in my apartment?
jokes.setup: The gang trapped in what they now know is the real killer's apartment
jokes.punchline: Gary's arrival creating immediate danger
jokes.explanation: Perfect cliffhanger ending - the meek hardware store guy is the real killer. Great callback and escalation.

tmdb_overview: After Mac arrives to the bar with scratches on his neck and exhibiting strange behavior, Frank comes to the conclusion that he is the serial killer terrorizing young women in Philadelphia. Frank sets out with his trusty chainsaw and a doubtful Charlie to find evidence to implicate Mac in the murders. In search of another suspect, Dennis and Dee do a little role-playing in order to help themselves better understand who they might be dealing with.
